commands.repair_reason: Human security/product review is needed because the PR reopens browser-origin silent pairing before generation-bound browser device tokens are implemented, and the external PR still lacks real behavior proof.; Needs attention: the diff reopens browser-origin silent device pairing without the acknowledged shared-auth-generation invalidation for browser-minted device tokens.; Review finding: [P1] Bind browser auto-pai... (sha=f5d46906d6170a0e202e128d44369879ec05c719)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway handshake pairing policy to silently pair fresh Control UI WebChat browser-origin clients with valid shared auth again, updates gateway auth regression tests, and rewrites the changelog note for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/81289.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection of the PR diff and current call site, the patched helper would allow Control UI WebChat browser-origin `not-paired` handshakes into the silent auto-approval path, and current tests show device-token sessions survive shared-token rotation.
commands.review_followup: Human security/product review is needed because the PR reopens browser-origin silent pairing before generation-bound browser device tokens are implemented, and the external PR still lacks real behavior proof.
